name: Bilbao Green Ring MTB Loop
name_original: Parque Venta Alta – circular por Gangoiti y Periurbano Arraizmedi desde Bibao
description: Explore the Parque Venta Alta – circular por Gangoiti y Periurbano Arraizmedi, a moderate 18.4-mile mountain biking route near Bilbao.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Bilbao offer a diverse topography, ranging from coastal areas to lush natural parks and mountainous terrain. The region is characterized by prominent mountains like Pagasarri and Artxanda, providing varied elevations and panoramic views. Dense forests, river valleys, and urban parks contribute to a rich landscape for mountain biking. This blend of natural features ensures a wide array of routes for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Bilbao The most popular mountain bike trail is Pico La Cruz – Path to Regato loop from Deustu, a 32.5 miles (52.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 53 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation gain and challenging terrain. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Plaza Nueva (Bilbao) – A & C Tunnel Entrance loop from Santutxu, a difficult 45.9 miles (73.8 km) path. This route offers a long-distance adventure through varied landscapes, including urban outskirts and natural areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Parque Artxanda a Club Artxanda Golf – circular desde Matiko, a 18.5 miles (29.8 km) trail leading through the hills and parks surrounding Bilbao, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around Bilbao is defined by its coastal proximity, natural parks, and prominent hills, offering a mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents. The network includes routes for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels, catering to a wide range of riders. The routes in Bilbao are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 3500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Bilbao's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Bilbao?
A: Bilbao offers a diverse network of over 60 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, ranging from easy paths to challenging climbs and descents across its varied terrain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails for all skill levels in Bilbao?
A: Yes, Bilbao's mountain biking scene caters to all abilities. There are 6 easy trails perfect for beginners, 27 moderate routes for those looking for a bit more challenge, and 27 difficult trails for experienced riders seeking demanding terrain and significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails near Bilbao?
A: The terrain around Bilbao is incredibly diverse, offering everything from coastal paths to lush natural parks and mountainous regions. You'll encounter prominent mountains like Pagasarri and Artxanda, dense forests, river valleys, and urban parks, providing a mix of challenging climbs, scenic descents, and varied surfaces.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Bilbao?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Bilbao are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1050751289/bilbao-green-ring-mtb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bilbao Green Ring MTB Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 18.4-mile (29.7 km) route that offers a great way to explore the city's green belt.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while mountain biking in Bilbao?
A: While mountain biking around Bilbao, you can encounter several impressive natural features and landmarks. The trails often lead through areas with panoramic views from peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Pagasarri&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/558197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ganekogorta Summit&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1516121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Butrón Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4018034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hirumugarrieta Megalithic Site&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in the Bilbao area?
A: While many trails in Bilbao are geared towards more experienced riders, there are country routes suitable for all skill levels, including families. For a moderate option, the Parque Venta Alta offers an 18.4-mile circular route through Gangoiti and Periurbano Arraizmedi, which is suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Bilbao?
A: The mountain biking trails in Bilbao are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 270 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse topography, the mix of challenging climbs and scenic descents, and the opportunity to explore both coastal areas and lush natural parks.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails with good views of Bilbao?
A: Absolutely. Mountains like Artxanda and Pagasarri, easily accessible from the city, offer numerous trails with rewarding panoramic views of Bilbao and its surrounding landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052082978/parque-artxanda-a-club-artxanda-golf-circular-desde-matiko" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Artxanda a Club Artxanda Golf – circular desde Matiko&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, leads through the hills surrounding Bilbao, providing excellent vistas.

Q: Are there any mountain bike parks or dedicated facilities in Bilbao?
A: Yes, El Vivero Bike Park, located in the hills near Bilbao, is a recognized bike park with well-maintained trails like Asuela, Sendero Central, and Aranzelai. It also provides facilities such as a cafe, toilets, water fountains, and picnic tables, making it a great destination for a dedicated mountain biking experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Bilbao?
A: Bilbao generally offers good mountain biking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and lush scenery. While winter can bring cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions, many trails remain accessible. Summer can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon rides are often preferred.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes around Bilbao?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer adventure, Bilbao offers several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24759719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Nueva (Bilbao) – A & C Tunnel Entrance loop from Santutxu&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 45.9-mile (73.8 km) path that provides a long-distance journey through varied landscapes, including urban outskirts and natural areas.

Q: Are there options for mountain biking near natural parks in the Bilbao region?
A: The Basque Country surrounding Bilbao is rich in natural parks, offering fantastic mountain biking opportunities. Urkiola Natural Park and Gorbeia Natural Park, both within a short drive, feature extensive forests, mountainous terrain, and diverse trails that are popular among mountain bikers and hikers alike.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this route as moderate, suggesting it's accessible to various skill levels but requires good fitness. However, some sections are considered more challenging, with other sources rating parts of the trail as 'Hard' or 'Difícil'. Be prepared for a demanding ride, especially given the nearly 30 km distance and 710 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse mix of surfaces. You'll encounter approximately 14.4 km of gravel, 11.1 km of asphalt, and 10.1 km of paved sections. Additionally, there are about 7.75 km of unpaved paths and 652 meters of natural terrain, providing a varied mountain biking experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Parque Venta Alta mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: On average, it takes about 2 hours and 36 minutes (9378 seconds) to complete this 29.7 km circular mountain bike route. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Parque Venta Alta mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Bilbao, making it easily accessible from the city. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, its proximity to Bilbao suggests various parking options within the city or near the starting point of the circular route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy several notable spots. Keep an eye out for the View of the Guggenheim Museum Bilbao, offering an iconic city vista. There's also a pleasant Picnic area in Gangoiti Forest, perfect for a break. The route also passes through the scenic Arraizmedi Periurban Park.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Venta Alta mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While there's no specific restriction mentioned, generally, dogs are welcome on trails in this region, especially if kept on a leash. Please ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to access the Parque Venta Alta – circular por Gangoiti y Periurbano Arraizmedi desde Bibao mountain bike route. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Basque Country generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking in spring and autumn, when temperatures are milder and the landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring colder, wetter weather. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or picnic spots along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a designated Picnic area in Gangoiti Forest, which is a great spot to take a break and refuel. For cafes or more extensive facilities, you would likely need to visit nearby towns or Bilbao itself.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginner mountain bikers?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' rating and sections described as 'hard' by some, this route is best suited for mountain bikers with good fitness and some experience. While parts may be manageable, the overall distance of nearly 30 km and 710 meters of elevation gain might be challenging for beginners or young families looking for a very easy ride.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths, including significant portions of the Bilboko Inguruko Berdegunea (34%) and Bilbao - Lutxana (13%). You'll also find smaller segments of the Northern Route (Basque Country), Goriko - Pagasarri, and Universidad de Deusto - Zorroza, among others.
